$comment: v0 tuning — beast_system.gd (VILLAGER-AND-GODHEAD-SPEC Part III, Beasts at Heel). All numbers here are given directly by the implementation brief (not independently invented): mealsByTier/ghalRankMealDiscount/minMeals/wildTierGate/unfedMoodDays/porterDayXp/trustDecayDays are the brief's own first guesses. trustDecayDays: a half-fed wild beast forgets you after 3 days without a meal. wildTierGate keys are WILD virtue scores (talent-soft-step/crab-friend/herd-sense thresholds in virtues.json), matched to tame.tier (1/2/3) in creatures.json — presentation passes the player's WILD score into can_tame(); the sim never reads AbilitiesSystem directly (systems stay decoupled, per CLAUDE.md).

bait.$comment: the Shepherd's Way, as VILLAGER-AND-GODHEAD-SPEC Part III §2 actually names it first: 'drop the species' craved food on the ground and stand off; the beast comes, eats, and trusts.' What shipped before this block was hand-feed-only ([E] with food in pack) — impossible for a chasing hound/eel-wolf, since AGGRO_RADIUS (170px, enemy.gd) outruns INTERACT_RANGE (56px, main.gd): you can never walk back in close enough to hand-feed something still hunting you. noticeRadius: how far a wild TAMEABLE beast (creature.tame non-empty) notices a matching dropped bait and breaks off whatever it was doing (chasing, attacking) to path to it instead — enemy.gd's own $comment marks exactly where this overrides the chase branch. eatRadius: how close it must close to actually eat (consumes the bait, calls beast_system.feed_wild for the dropper). calmSeconds: reuses enemy.gd's existing pacify() so a fed-but-not-yet-tamed beast doesn't instantly resume the chase — the loop should read as trust, not a feeding-frenzy exploit. spoilAtDawn: uneaten bait vanishes at the next dawn (main.gd's own dawn step, same shape as beast_dawn_feeding) rather than littering the flats forever.

xp.$comment: the curve is villagers.json xp — one growth system, three wearers (player virtues / villager Arms / beast levels). beast_system.gd reads tuning/villagers.json's xp.curveConstant + xp.maxLevel directly; nothing is duplicated here.
